entities.name: 80/20 Polarization Framework
entities.context: Training philosophy where ~80% of workouts are low intensity (Zone 2) and ~20% are high intensity intervals, enforced by the workout validator

entities.name: Zone 2 (Z2)
entities.context: Low-intensity heart rate zone used as the base aerobic training target in the 80/20 framework

entities.name: Zone 4 (Z4)
entities.context: High-intensity heart rate zone threshold used to detect interval workout adherence

entities.name: The Gray Zone
entities.context: Anti-pattern where workout intensity is too hard for Zone 2 but too easy for intervals — represents failed adherence to polarized training

entities.name: The 20% Interval
entities.context: Archetype label for workouts that hit high-intensity targets, representing the interval portion of 80/20 training

entities.name: Workout Validator
entities.context: Python script (validate_workout) that checks daily workout data against polarized training targets, classifying workouts by archetype and adherence status

entities.name: Heart Rate Zone System
entities.context: Dictionary-based zone definitions (ZONES) used to classify workout intensity by average heart rate

relationships.context: The validator enforces adherence to the 80/20 polarized training model by classifying each workout
The Gray Zone is the failure mode of polarized training — workouts that violate the principle by falling between zones
Interval archetype is detected when average HR approaches Z4 threshold (Z4 lower bound minus 5 bpm)
Validator reads ZONES dictionary to determine HR thresholds for archetype classification
Zone 2 represents the 80% low-intensity base of the polarized model
Zone 4 represents the 20% high-intensity portion of the polarized model
